grammar.title: What and Who: Choose the Question Word
grammar.explanation: Use what to ask about a thing, action or job. Use who to ask about a person.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
What is Selma doing? She is driving the bus.
Who is driving the bus? Selma is driving the bus.
grammar.commonMistake:
Mistake: Who is the firefighter doing?
Fix: What is the firefighter doing?
grammar.memoryTip: Who asks for a person. What asks for information about a thing or action.
grammar.practice: Choose What or Who: ___ is the bus driver? | ___ is the doctor doing? | ___ is helping Samira? | ___ does a firefighter wear? | ___ is your teacher?

grammar.title: What and Who: Build the Question
grammar.explanation: Begin with What or Who, keep the words in question order, and finish with a question mark.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
Who helps sick people? A doctor helps sick people.
What is the farmer growing? The farmer is growing maize.
grammar.commonMistake:
Mistake: Who the bus drives?
Fix: Who drives the bus?
grammar.memoryTip: Question word first, question mark last.
grammar.practice: Build one What question and one Who question for each scene: a firefighter using a hose; a nurse helping a child; a farmer growing beans.

grammar.title: Actions Now: Choose Is or Are
grammar.explanation: Use the present continuous for an action happening now. Use is with one person or thing and are with more than one.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
The driver is waving.
The firefighters are climbing.
We are helping our neighbours.
grammar.commonMistake:
Mistake: The firefighters is climbing.
Fix: The firefighters are climbing.
grammar.memoryTip: One is; more than one are.
grammar.practice: Complete with is or are: The officer ___ helping. | The children ___ counting. | Samira ___ watching. | We ___ learning. | The farmers ___ growing maize.

grammar.title: Action Words: Add -ing
grammar.explanation: Add -ing to many action words to show that the action is happening now. Some words change before -ing is added.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
help + ing = helping
drive - e + ing = driving
run + n + ing = running
grammar.commonMistake:
Mistake: driveing.
Fix: driving. Drop the final e before adding -ing.
grammar.memoryTip: Look at the action word before you add -ing.
grammar.practice: Write the -ing form: help | teach | rescue | drive | clean | run.

grammar.title: Actions Now: Build Complete Sentences
grammar.explanation: A complete present-continuous sentence needs a subject, is or are, and an action word ending in -ing.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
The window cleaner is cleaning the glass.
The neighbours are carrying boxes.
grammar.commonMistake:
Mistake: The nurse helping the child.
Fix: The nurse is helping the child.
grammar.practice: Put the words in order: is / Ishmael / cleaning / the window | are / the firefighters / water / using | helping / Samira and Hooyo / are / Samira.

grammar.title: Neighbourhood Reporter
grammar.explanation: Use the present continuous to report what different people are doing in a busy neighbourhood scene.
grammar.ruleAndExamples: Selma is driving the bus. Two firefighters are putting out the fire. Adam is keeping people safe.
grammar.commonMistake: Check that is or are matches the person or people you name.
grammar.memoryTip: Look, name, choose is or are, then say the action.
grammar.practice: Describe six actions in the neighbourhood picture. Use at least three sentences with is and two sentences with are.

speakingAdditions.title: Speaking 4 - Who and What Detective
speakingAdditions.instructionsAndModelLines: Look at a neighbourhood scene. Ask three Who questions and three What questions. Model: Who is driving the bus? Selma is driving. What is Ishmael doing? He is cleaning a window.
speakingAdditions.aiTutorPrompt: Answer my six neighbourhood questions, then tell me whether I used Who and What correctly.

speakingAdditions.title: Speaking 5 - What Is Happening Now?
speakingAdditions.instructionsAndModelLines: Describe six actions in the picture using is or are and an -ing word. Model: The firefighter is carrying a hose. The neighbours are helping Samira.
speakingAdditions.aiTutorPrompt: Listen to my six action sentences. Check my use of is, are and -ing.

speakingAdditions.title: Speaking 6 - Be a Good Neighbour
speakingAdditions.instructionsAndModelLines: Role-play noticing that a neighbour needs help. Ask what happened, offer help and respond politely. Model: What happened? Your box is heavy. I can help you carry it. Thank you, good neighbour!
speakingAdditions.aiTutorPrompt: Role-play a neighbour who needs simple help, then give me feedback on my questions and kind response.

writingAdditions.title: Writing 4 - A Community Helper Card
writingAdditions.promptAndInstructions: Create a fact card about one community helper. Name the job, explain two duties, and name two clothes or tools used for the job.
writingAdditions.modelText: A firefighter helps people. A firefighter puts out fires and rescues people. Firefighters wear helmets and boots. They use a hose and ladder.
writingAdditions.successCriteria: I named the job; explained two duties; named two clothes or tools; used complete sentences and punctuation
writingAdditions.support: Use the jobs-and-equipment word bank and the model card.
writingAdditions.extension: Add a sentence explaining why the job is important.

writingAdditions.title: Writing 5 - Warta Street News Report
writingAdditions.promptAndInstructions: Write a short report about the fire on Warta Street. Tell where it happened, who came to help, what people did and how the event ended.
writingAdditions.modelText: A fire started near the market on Warta Street. Firefighters came in a red fire engine. They put out the fire and rescued the goats. The neighbours helped Samira clean her shop. Everyone was safe in the end.
writingAdditions.successCriteria: I explained where, who, what and the ending; used story facts; put events in order; checked capitals and punctuation
writingAdditions.support: Use the story, the sequence words first, next and finally, and the model report.
writingAdditions.extension: Add one quotation from a character.

writingAdditions.title: Writing 6 - My Good-Neighbour Promise
writingAdditions.promptAndInstructions: Write six sentences about how you can be a good neighbour at home, at school or on your street. Include one action you can do this week.
writingAdditions.modelText: I can greet my neighbours kindly. I can carry a bag for an older person. I can keep our shared space clean. I can listen when someone needs help. This week, I will help sweep near our door. Good neighbours work together.
writingAdditions.sentenceStarter: I can be a good neighbour by
writingAdditions.successCriteria: I wrote six relevant promises; included one action for this week; used clear verbs; checked complete sentences
writingAdditions.support: Use I can and I will sentence starters.
writingAdditions.extension: Explain how one promise will help another person.

activityAdditions.title: Activity 5 - Who Uses It?
activityAdditions.instructionsAndItems: Sort each item under the correct helper: hose | helmet | stethoscope | medicine | books | board | badge | whistle | seeds | tractor.
activityAdditions.answerSummary: Firefighter: hose, helmet; doctor: stethoscope, medicine; teacher: books, board; police officer: badge, whistle; farmer: seeds, tractor.

activityAdditions.title: Activity 6 - Neighbourhood Action Hunt
activityAdditions.instructionsAndItems: Find or imagine six people helping in a neighbourhood. For each person, record the job and one action happening now using is or are + verb-ing.
activityAdditions.answerSummary: Answers vary; require six sensible job-and-action pairs with correct is/are + verb-ing sentences.

quizAdditions.question: Who helps people who are sick?
quizAdditions.options: A doctor | A farmer | A bus driver | A reporter
quizAdditions.explanation: A doctor helps people who are sick.

quizAdditions.question: Which question asks about an action?
quizAdditions.options: What is the firefighter doing? | Who is the firefighter? | Who is your neighbour? | Who drives the bus?
quizAdditions.explanation: Use What to ask about an action.

quizAdditions.question: The firefighters ___ climbing the ladder.
quizAdditions.explanation: Firefighters means more than one person, so use are.

quizAdditions.question: Which is the correct -ing form of drive?
quizAdditions.explanation: Drop the final e in drive before adding -ing.

quizAdditions.question: Which clothes protect a firefighter's head and feet?
quizAdditions.options: A helmet and boots | A mask and gloves | A uniform and badge | A coat and scarf
quizAdditions.explanation: A helmet protects the head and strong boots protect the feet.

liveSessions.title: Meet the neighbourhood helpers
liveSessions.beforeSession: Watch the teacher vocabulary lecture and learn the People in Your Neighbourhood group.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min welcome; 10 min picture talk; 10 min vocabulary retrieval; 15 min describe-a-job speaking; 5 min reflection
liveSessions.afterSession: Complete Speaking 1 and add five helpers to My Word Book.

liveSessions.title: Firefighters, equipment and safety
liveSessions.beforeSession: Listen to Firefighters and Their Equipment and bring one safety question.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min retrieval; 10 min shared reading; 10 min equipment sorting; 15 min comprehension discussion; 5 min safety exit ticket
liveSessions.afterSession: Complete Activity 4 and improve two reading answers.

liveSessions.title: Ask with What and Who
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min warm-up; 10 min What/Who sort; 10 min teacher modelling; 15 min question detective speaking; 5 min feedback
liveSessions.afterSession: Record Speaking 4 and correct one question.

liveSessions.title: Actions happening now
liveSessions.beforeSession: Complete Grammar Practices 3-6 and practise the action words.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min action mime; 10 min is/are check; 10 min build -ing words; 15 min neighbourhood reporting; 5 min review

liveSessions.title: The Helpers of Warta Street
liveSessions.beforeSession: Listen to the story and choose one helper you admire.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min prediction; 10 min echo reading; 10 min event sequencing; 15 min Warta Street news writing; 5 min peer feedback

liveSessions.title: Good-neighbour showcase and review
liveSessions.beforeSession: Complete the ten-question quiz and bring one portfolio piece.
liveSessions.agenda: 10 min retrieval game; 10 min quiz corrections; 10 min role-plays; 10 min portfolio shares; 5 min self-assessment
liveSessions.afterSession: Complete Writing 6 and save the final self-assessment.
